_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 3-6 Hyperactive Delirium Syndrome with Severe Agitation (Revised 05/26/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured. Patients > 14 years of age.
condition: Hyperactive Delirium Syndrome with Severe Agitation
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Treatment Protocol Section 3-6: Hyperactive Delirium Syndrome with Severe Agitation — MDHHS Approved 5/26/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Indications: patient > 14 years of age who is an imminent physical threat to personnel and/or themselves and level of agitation is such that transport may place all parties at risk. Hyperactive delirium syndrome with severe agitation is a life-threatening constellation of symptoms including severe agitation and vital sign abnormalities (tachycardia, hyperthermia).

sections.title: Hyperactive Delirium — Treatment
points: Ensure appropriate personnel available to provide patient and provider safety — refer to Patient Restraint-Procedure Protocol

Obtain history when possible; perform visual patient assessment looking for cause of behavior (visible trauma, stroke symptoms, etc.) — if alternate cause of behavior is likely, transition to Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol or other applicable protocol

For patients who are uncontrollably agitated despite de-escalation techniques: prepare for airway management and administer per MCA selection — EITHER ketamine 4 mg/kg IM (maximum single dose 500 mg; 3–5 minute onset) OR midazolam 10 mg IM/IN (Specialist skill)
Once adequate sedation is obtained: continuously monitor SpO2; monitor capnometry per End Tidal Carbon Dioxide Monitoring-Procedure Protocol; obtain temperature
If hyperthermic (temp > 38°C or 100.4°F): provide cooling via ice packs to neck, axilla, groin and/or fluids to skin while promoting evaporation (air movement)

Establish IV per Vascular Access and IV Therapy-Procedure Protocol — provide fluid bolus of up to 1 L of NS or LR; reassess patient — repeat boluses to maximum 2 L NS or LR as long as vital sign abnormalities persist (Specialist skill)
Monitor for pulmonary edema — if pulmonary edema presents, stop fluids and contact Medical Control for direction
Consider 12-lead if any evidence of hyperkalemia (peaked T waves, prolonged PR, widened QRS) — 12-lead per MCA selection (may be BLS or Specialist) per 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol
Continuously monitor patient for potential need of airway management and treatment of hemodynamic compromise
Contact Medical Control if additional sedation is required

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select sedation agent for hyperactive delirium with severe agitation — select ONE or both; must select at least one
options: Ketamine 4 mg/kg IM (max single dose 500 mg)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select whether 12-lead ECG is a BLS or Specialist procedure

key_drugs.name: Ketamine IM (Hyperactive Delirium)
key_drugs.dose: 4 mg/kg IM — maximum single dose 500 mg (3–5 minute onset)
key_drugs.indication: Hyperactive delirium syndrome with severe agitation unresponsive to de-escalation
key_drugs.contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to ketamine; use with caution in severe hypertension or known thyroid disease
key_drugs.side_effects: Laryngospasm (rare), emergence reactions, hypersalivation, transient hypertension and tachycardia, temporary respiratory depression
key_drugs.notes: 3–5 minute onset IM. Prepare airway management before administering. Monitor SpO2 and ETCO2 continuously after administration.

key_drugs.name: Midazolam 10 mg IM/IN (Hyperactive Delirium)
key_drugs.indication: Hyperactive delirium syndrome with severe agitation unresponsive to de-escalation
key_drugs.notes: Monitor airway closely. Prepare for airway management. Contact Medical Control if additional sedation required.

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV Fluid Bolus
key_drugs.dose: Up to 1 liter — repeat to maximum 2 L as long as vital sign abnormalities persist
key_drugs.indication: Hyperactive delirium with hemodynamic compromise after sedation
key_drugs.contraindications: Signs of pulmonary edema — stop fluids; contact Medical Control
key_drugs.notes: Monitor for pulmonary edema during and after administration.

deterioration_flags: Respiratory compromise after sedation — airway management priority; BVM if SpO2 < 90%; prepare for advanced airway
Hyperthermia (> 38°C / 100.4°F) — aggressive cooling (ice packs to neck, axilla, groin; evaporative cooling); this is a life-threatening feature of hyperactive delirium
ECG changes suggesting hyperkalemia (peaked T waves, prolonged PR, widened QRS) — 12-lead ECG; consider calcium chloride (Specialist skill); contact Medical Control
Hemodynamic instability post-sedation — IV fluid bolus (Specialist skill); contact Medical Control for vasopressor guidance
scope_notes: De-escalation, physical restraint (with appropriate personnel): all levels
Ketamine IM or midazolam IM/IN (per MCA selection): Specialist skill
SpO2 and ETCO2 monitoring after sedation: Specialist skill
IV access and fluid bolus: Specialist skill
Cardiac monitoring and 12-lead ECG: Specialist skill; 12-lead may be BLS per MCA selection
